searches_performed.query: vitamin K warfarin INR dietary intake randomized controlled trial pharmacokinetic interaction
searches_performed.notes: Key sources: Violi 2016 systematic review, Sconce 2007 and Rombouts 2007 RCTs on low-dose K stabilization.

sources.title: Interaction Between Dietary Vitamin K Intake and Anticoagulation by Vitamin K Antagonists: Is It Really True? A Systematic Review
sources.design_notes: Reviews observational + interventional studies on dietary vitamin K and warfarin stability.
sources.key_findings: Conflicting evidence on whether dietary vitamin K intake affects warfarin response. Some studies show inverse correlation between vitamin K intake and INR changes. Others suggest a minimum vitamin K intake is REQUIRED for stable anticoagulation. Conclusion: available evidence does NOT support the old advice to restrict dietary vitamin K when starting warfarin. Consistency of intake matters more than absolute level.

sources.title: Effect of fixed-dose vitamin K supplementation on INR stability in warfarin patients
sources.population: Warfarin-treated patients with unstable anticoagulation control
sources.design_notes: Randomized 70 patients to daily 150 microgram oral vitamin K supplementation vs placebo for 6 months.
sources.key_findings: Daily low-dose vitamin K supplementation resulted in significantly greater decreases in INR standard deviation and greater increases in percentage time within target INR range. Low-dose vitamin K can STABILIZE anticoagulation in patients with variable response.

sources.title: Dietary vitamin K variability affects International Normalized Ratio (INR) coagulation indices
sources.design_notes: Tracked daily vitamin K intake via food diaries alongside INR.
sources.key_findings: Inverse correlation between variability in INR and changes in vitamin K intake (r = -0.600, p < 0.01). VARIABILITY is the issue, not absolute level.

claims.statement: Vitamin K directly antagonizes warfarin by providing the substrate warfarin inhibits; large swings in vitamin K intake destabilize INR.

dose_threshold.a: Dose varies by source (leafy greens, K1/K2 supplements). Clinical impact depends on CHANGE from baseline, not absolute.
dose_threshold.notes: Warfarin dose may need re-titration if vitamin K intake changes by >30% from stable baseline.

claims.statement: Consistent vitamin K intake produces stable INR; the old 'avoid all green vegetables' advice is not supported by current evidence.

dose_threshold.a: Low-dose supplementation (150 microgram/day) can stabilize unstable patients (S2).

claims.statement: Patients on warfarin should inform their anticoagulation clinic before starting or stopping vitamin K supplements, and should aim for consistent dietary vitamin K intake rather than avoidance.

dose_threshold.a: Any supplemental dose warrants INR monitoring.
dose_threshold.notes: INR check within 1-2 weeks of any intake change is standard.

contradictions.topic: Low-dose vitamin K supplementation: destabilizer or stabilizer?
contradictions.resolution: Paradoxically, low-dose daily vitamin K (150 microgram) STABILIZES INR in previously unstable patients by flattening day-to-day intake variability. This reframes the interaction from 'avoid K' to 'maintain consistency.' Draft reflects this honestly.

reviewer_notes: Original INTERACTIONS entry was DANGER, 31 words, 1/3 specific. Severity downgraded to WARNING. The mechanism is real and strong, but the clinical implication is 'consistent intake + INR monitoring' not 'avoid at all costs.' DANGER overstates and gives outdated advice; WARNING correctly signals meaningful impact with clear mitigation.
